stories.name: database-schema-and-migrations
stories.title: Implement database schema for media support
stories.description: Create database migration adding `media_blobs` table, `message_media` junction table, and `media_json` column to `nostr_messages`. This provides the foundational data layer for content-addressed blob storage and message-media associations.

includes: Migration file 20260213_add_media_support.ts
media_blobs table with hash PK, mime_type, size_bytes, dimensions_json, blurhash, local_path, uploaded_at, created_at
message_media junction table with FKs, remote_url, placeholder_key, upload_status
Index on message_media.upload_status
media_json column on nostr_messages table
Migration rollback support
excludes: Data access layer or repository classes
Actual file I/O or blob storage

stories.notes: Foundation story with no dependencies. Testable via direct database schema queries after migration.

stories.name: local-blob-storage-and-hashing
stories.title: Implement local blob storage with streaming SHA-256 hashing
stories.description: Implement content-addressed blob storage at {userData}/blobs/ with streaming SHA-256 hashing. Includes file persistence keyed by hash, metadata extraction (MIME type, size, dimensions, blurhash for images), and EXIF stripping for images. Deduplication is automatic via hash-based storage.

includes: Streaming SHA-256 hasher (no full-file buffering)
Blob storage service (save, retrieve, delete by hash)
Storage location {userData}/blobs/<hash>
MIME type detection
Image dimension extraction
Blurhash generation for images only
Mandatory EXIF stripping with failure handling
Database persistence to media_blobs table
Deduplication via hash-keyed storage
excludes: Storage cleanup and quota enforcement (separate story)

stories.notes: Can be tested independently with known test files. Verify hash accuracy, storage location, metadata extraction, and EXIF stripping with exiftool.

stories.name: blossom-server-configuration
stories.title: Implement per-identity Blossom server configuration
stories.description: Add per-identity Blossom server list configuration with UI for adding, removing, and reordering servers. Includes persistence, health check (HEAD request with 3s timeout), and server fallback logic. No default servers - users must configure manually.

includes: Database schema for per-identity server list (URL + label)
Identity settings UI section for Blossom servers
Add/remove/reorder server UI controls
Server health check (HEAD request, 3s timeout)
Server selection and fallback logic
TLS requirement validation (reject http:// URLs)
Clear prompts when no server configured
Persistence across restarts

test_fixtures.description: Local HTTP server for health check testing
test_fixtures.interface: HTTP server responding to HEAD requests
test_fixtures.reason: Test health check and fallback logic without real Blossom infrastructure

stories.notes: Testable with test HTTP servers simulating healthy/unhealthy endpoints. Verify persistence via DB queries and UI state after restart.

stories.name: nip98-authentication
stories.title: Implement NIP-98 HTTP authentication for Blossom uploads
stories.description: Implement NIP-98 authentication token generation for Blossom HTTP requests. Generates kind 27235 Nostr events signed by active identity containing HTTP method, URL, and body hash. Tokens are short-lived (60s validity) and generated per-request.

includes: NIP-98 token generator (kind 27235 event)
Event tags with HTTP method, URL, SHA-256 body hash
Event signing with active identity key
Base64 Authorization header formatting
60-second validity window enforcement
Per-request token generation (no caching)

stories.notes: Unit testable with mock identity keys. Verify token structure, signature validity, timestamp freshness, and uniqueness across requests.

stories.name: attachment-ui-file-selection
stories.title: Implement attachment UI with file selection and validation
stories.description: Add paperclip button to message input, file picker dialog integration, drag-and-drop support, and file validation (size limit 25 MB, supported MIME types). Includes preview strip with thumbnails/icons, file metadata display, and remove buttons. Does not send messages yet - focuses on attachment preparation.

includes: Paperclip button in message input area
Button disabled state (matches Send button conditions)
OS native file picker integration
Drag-and-drop zone with visual feedback
File size validation (25 MB default max)
MIME type validation with supported types list
Error toasts for rejection (size/type)
Attachment preview strip UI component
Image thumbnail generation (max 120px height)
MIME type icon for non-images
File name and size display
Remove (X) button per attachment
Multiple attachment support with horizontal scroll
Integration with blob storage (trigger hash + save)

stories.notes: Testable via Playwright in dual-instance environment. Verify button states, file picker, drag-and-drop, validation rejections, and preview display. Use browser_take_screenshot for visual verification.

stories.name: message-construction-with-placeholders
stories.title: Implement message construction with local-blob placeholders
stories.description: Extend message send flow to construct Nostr events with imeta tags containing local-blob:<sha256> placeholders. Store message-media associations and queue messages with pending uploads. Text input remains functional for captions alongside attachments.

includes: Send button triggers message construction with attachments
Enter key support for send with attachments
NIP-94 imeta tag generation per attachment
Placeholder format: url local-blob:<sha256>
Metadata tags: m (MIME), size, dim (images), blurhash (images), sha256
Attachment ordering preservation in imeta tags
media_json column population in nostr_messages
message_media junction table entries
Message queued with status queued
Preview strip clears after send
Caption support (message body alongside attachments)

stories.notes: Testable by verifying queued message structure in database. Confirm imeta tags, placeholders, metadata, and junction table associations. Use test files with known hashes.

stories.name: upload-pipeline-blossom-integration
stories.title: Implement upload pipeline with Blossom BUD-06 integration
stories.description: Implement blob upload to Blossom servers using HTTP PUT with NIP-98 authentication. Includes upload timing (immediate if online, deferred if offline), placeholder replacement on success, retry logic with exponential backoff, partial failure handling, concurrency limiting (2 per identity), and IPC progress reporting. Integrates with existing message queue flush logic.

includes: Upload trigger on Send (if online) or connectivity restore
BUD-06 HTTP PUT upload with NIP-98 Authorization header
Server health check before upload attempt
URL extraction from server response
Placeholder replacement in queued event (local-blob -> remote URL)
message_media.remote_url update
Retry with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 30s max, 5 attempts)
Per-attachment upload status tracking
Partial failure handling (message not published until all uploads succeed)
Retry only failed attachments on manual retry
Concurrency limit (2 uploads per identity)
Upload cancellation support
Resume after app restart (queued state persistence)
IPC progress updates (nostling:media:upload-progress)
Integration with existing queue flush logic (extension not replacement)
Server rejection error messages (413, 415 HTTP codes)

test_fixtures.description: Local BUD-06 compliant Blossom server for upload testing
test_fixtures.interface: HTTP server implementing BUD-06 PUT endpoint with NIP-98 verification
test_fixtures.reason: Cannot use production Blossom servers in CI/CD - need controllable test environment with failure simulation

stories.notes: High complexity due to retry logic, concurrency, and queue integration. Use local Blossom test server with configurable responses (success, timeout, rejection). Verify upload timing, NIP-98 headers, retry intervals, partial failure, concurrency limits, and cancellation.

stories.name: encryption-and-publish-integration
stories.title: Integrate placeholder resolution with NIP-17/59 encryption and publish
stories.description: Ensure placeholders are replaced before NIP-17/59 encryption and publish. Validate that no local-blob: placeholders appear in published relay events. Messages remain queued until all uploads complete and placeholders are resolved.

includes: Pre-publish validation: all placeholders must be resolved
Integration with existing NIP-17/59 encryption flow
Publish only after all uploads succeed and URLs replaced
Abort publish if any placeholder remains (safety check)
Update message status to sent after successful publish
imeta tags encrypted inside NIP-59 sealed event alongside message content

stories.notes: Critical constraint story - MUST verify no placeholder leakage. Use relay inspector in dual-instance environment to capture published events, decrypt, and confirm real URLs only. Can test independently by mocking upload completion.

stories.name: media-rendering-in-conversation
stories.title: Implement media rendering for outgoing and incoming messages
stories.description: Render media attachments in conversation UI with proper state handling. Outgoing: local preview from blob storage, upload progress bar, status transitions (queued → uploading → sending → sent → error), retry button on error. Incoming: fetch from URL, cache in existing image cache, blurhash placeholder, click-to-expand lightbox for images, download links for non-images. Support both NIP-94 imeta and legacy NIP-92 url tags.

includes: Outgoing message bubble with local blob preview
Upload progress bar overlay on thumbnail
Status icon transitions (clock → progress → spinner → checkmark → warning)
Retry button on error state
Inline image thumbnail for sent messages
Click-to-expand lightbox for images
Download link for non-image files (name, size, MIME icon)
Incoming media fetch from URL in imeta tags
URL-keyed cache for received media (existing image cache)
Blurhash placeholder rendering while loading/offline
NIP-92 url tag parsing for backward compatibility
NIP-94 imeta tag parsing

stories.notes: Complex UI story with multiple states. Test with dual-instance environment: Instance A sends with attachment, verify local preview and upload progress. Instance B receives, verify fetch, cache, and blurhash. Use browser_take_screenshot for state verification.

stories.name: storage-cleanup-and-quota-enforcement
stories.title: Implement blob storage cleanup and quota enforcement
stories.description: Implement blob cleanup policy: remove uploaded blobs after 7-day retention grace period (configurable). Enforce storage quota (default 500 MB, configurable) with LRU eviction of already-uploaded blobs. Retain blobs referenced by multiple identities until all references resolved.

includes: Cleanup scheduler (triggered periodically or on-demand)
7-day retention grace period after upload (configurable)
Uploaded blob deletion from disk and media_blobs table
Storage quota calculation (total blob storage size)
LRU eviction of uploaded blobs when quota exceeded
Multi-identity blob retention (retain if any identity still references)
Never evict unuploaded (queued) blobs
Configuration for retention period and quota limit
IPC channel for manual cleanup trigger (nostling:media:cleanup)

stories.notes: Testable by fast-forwarding time or manually triggering cleanup. Verify blob deletion after retention period, quota enforcement, LRU ordering, and multi-identity retention. Use low quota (10 MB) for easier testing.

dependency_notes: Stories are designed to be independent vertical slices. Story 01 (database schema) is the foundation but other stories can proceed in parallel using the schema definition. Story 07 (upload pipeline) is the most complex and integrates components from Stories 02 (blob storage), 03 (server config), and 04 (NIP-98 auth), but can be implemented and tested independently using test fixtures for the Blossom server. Story 08 (encryption integration) depends on upload completion events but uses the existing encryption implementation. Story 09 (UI rendering) can be developed in parallel with backend stories by using test data with various message states.
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks used. External dependencies (Blossom server, HTTP endpoints) are handled via permanent test fixtures (FIXTURE-03-001, FIXTURE-07-001) which provide BUD-06 compliant test doubles for CI/CD and development. These fixtures are permanent testing infrastructure and do not require resolution.
